hypothesis: If molecular generation is steered by adding concept-based activation vectors—derived from a frozen Concept Bottleneck Model trained to disentangle property-specific representations within a frozen LLM's hidden space—then the resulting molecules will more accurately satisfy target physicochemical property constraints (e.g., QED and LogP) compared to baseline steering and fine-tuning approaches, because disentangled, property-orthogonal steering vectors provide more precise and interpretable control over the generative process without degrading molecular validity or diversity.
